turns.content: What brings you in today?

Well, I've had this cough that just won't go away for about two weeks now. It's not really a deep, phlegmy cough — more of a dry, tickly thing that comes and goes. But the part that worries me is sometimes, especially when I'm walking up the stairs at school, I feel like I just can't get enough air. I'm a teacher, so I'm on my feet a lot, and that's really been bothering me.
When did this start?

It started about two weeks ago, just a mild cough after what I thought was a cold. But the cough never really went away, and then maybe in the last week or so I noticed that feeling of being out of breath when I climb stairs. It's gotten a little worse, not better.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

There's no sharp pain, really. It's more of a tightness or pressure right in the middle of my chest when I'm trying to catch my breath. Not like a burning or stabbing, just a heavy, uncomfortable feeling that makes me want to stop and rest until it passes. The cough itself feels like it's coming from my throat, not deep in my lungs.
Have you had any fever or chills?

No, I haven't had any fever or chills at all. I even checked my temperature a couple times just to be sure, and it was always normal. I've felt a little warm at night sometimes, but nothing like a proper fever.
What medications are you currently taking?

I'm not taking any prescription medications right now. I did try some over-the-counter cough syrup a few days ago, but it didn't really help. I've also been drinking a lot of tea with honey, just trying to soothe my throat. That's about it.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?

No, I don't have any chronic medical conditions. I'm generally pretty healthy, aside from the occasional cold or sinus infection like everyone else. I don't have asthma, diabetes, or anything like that.
Are you allergic to any medications?

Not that I'm aware of. I've taken antibiotics before, like amoxicillin for a sinus infection a few years ago, and I didn't have any reaction. I've never had a problem with any other medication that I know of.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

Just the over-the-counter cough syrup and tea with honey I mentioned. I've also tried using a humidifier in my bedroom at night, thinking maybe the dry air was making the cough worse, but I'm not sure if it helped much. I haven't taken anything else or seen anyone else about this.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Resting definitely helps—if I sit down for a few minutes after climbing stairs, the tightness in my chest goes away. But as for the cough, it seems worse when I'm talking a lot during the school day or if I laugh too hard. I haven't really noticed it being worse after eating or lying down, but I also haven't paid that close attention.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's been pretty frustrating, honestly. At school, I have to keep pausing when I walk through the hallways or up the stairs, and I've had to stop a few times in the middle of teaching a lesson to catch my breath. That's not like me at all. The cough also interrupts me when I'm trying to talk to my students, which makes
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
No, I don't smoke at all, and I only have a glass of wine maybe once or twice a month, if that. So nothing regular.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

Well, my father had asthma when he was younger, but he grew out of it as an adult. No one else in my family has had anything like this that I know of. I'm not sure if that's related, but I thought I'd mention it.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

No, I've never had anything like this before. I've had the occasional cold that hung around for a week or so, but never a cough that lasted two weeks, and I've definitely never had that feeling of not being able to catch my breath like this. It's really new for me.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?

Yes, that makes sense. Thank you for explaining everything so clearly. I just want to make sure I'm not missing anything important, since this is all new to me. Should I be worried about what's causing this?
